architecture_fit.note: Solves addition exactly, but carry propagation — the goal's hard part — is a hand-set Python ripple loop, not an attention/transformer mechanism; only digit routing is actually attention.

baseline_comparison.note: Clean strawman: the no-carry linear baseline scores 0.0 exact-match on all carrying slices while the full circuit hits 1.0.

faithfulness.note: Built-in causal ablation: zeroing the carry channel collapses the circuit exactly to the linear baseline; somewhat tautological since weights are hand-set, but it's the right test, cleanly done.

operating_range.note: Exact across all 4 carry slices and 8 held-out seeds; data-independent so no breakdown shown, but operand range 0–999 and full carry-chain length are covered.

hardcoded_weights_bonus.note: Entire mechanism is hand-constructed (value embedding + hand-set attention routing + explicit carry channel), exactly the strong evidence-of-understanding this bonus rewards.

visual_judgement.note: Demo pairs an interactive per-example carry-ripple probe with a full-vs-ablated exact-match bar chart; the claim is legible without the README.

visualisation_rationale.note: README justifies exact-match on the y-axis against the carry-count axis the goal hinges on, contrasting full vs carry-ablated as the minimal flip-the-claim artefact.

notes: A clean, honest hand-built circuit that aces every carry slice across seeds with a strong no-carry baseline contrast and a built-in causal ablation, earning the hardcoded-weights bonus. Its main weakness is architecture fit: carry propagation — the very inter-position routing the goal asks attention to perform — is implemented as an explicit Python ripple loop rather than an attention/MLP mechanism, so only digit fetching is genuinely attentional. Mean of scored items (~4.1) places it solidly in the 'good' band.
